topic.what: batch-puncturing ciphertext-policy attribute-based encryption (BPCP-ABE) for circuits
topic.how: using evasive LWE and tensor LWE assumptions with novel randomized dual-tier keys and approximate homomorphic computation techniques
topic.why_matters: enables fine-grained forward security with constant-size punctured keys independent of number of punctured tags or circuit depth/size, solving key size growth problem in existing puncturable ABE

characteristics.what_it_measures: size of punctured secret key relative to number of punctured tags
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 comparison; punctured key size independent of η (number of punctured tags) and d (circuit depth)
characteristics.context: constant-size punctured key regardless of batch size

characteristics.vs_baseline: false for bilinear group schemes (GM15, SSSLG20, PNXW18)
characteristics.evidence: based on LWE and variants; Table 1 column PQ
characteristics.context: first lattice-based batch-puncturing CP-ABE for circuits

characteristics.what_it_measures: ability to revoke multiple tags in single puncture operation
characteristics.vs_baseline: false for all prior PE and puncturable ABE schemes
characteristics.evidence: BatchPun algorithm allows multiple tags {t'_η,1,...,t'_η,kη}; Table 1 column BP
characteristics.context: number of tags punctured each time freely determined by users

characteristics.vs_baseline: NC1 for pairing-based CP-ABE; P only for KP-ABE from lattices
characteristics.evidence: scheme supports circuits class F={f:Z_ℓ^q→{0,1}}; Wee [41] first lattice-based CP-ABE for circuits
characteristics.context: richest function class from lattices for CP-ABE

characteristics.what_it_measures: punctured key size independence from parameters
characteristics.evidence: Section 3 and Table 1; size of psk independent of number of punctured tags, circuit depth, and circuit size
characteristics.context: solves the key size growth problem in existing puncturable ABE

characteristics.evidence: Section 4 and Theorem 1; uses modified evasive LWE (Remark 1) and matrix variant of tensor LWE (Lemma 3)
characteristics.context: strong but falsifiable assumptions from evasive and tensor LWE
